action.reasoning: Observation: Best LPIPS=0.6021 (marginal gain); inspect shows renderer.forward requires decoder first then triplane/rays_o/rays_d; all tested variants in inspect failed due to wrong order/args (no decoder provided); fallback to orbit render explains persistently high LPIPS (pose mismatch on varying target az~185° rel). Analysis: Custom rays_o/rays_d func is correct per source but renderer invocation was incomplete; correct call model.renderer(model.decoder, scene_codes, rays_o, rays_d) will return dict (likely with 'rgb'/'comp_rgb' key); white-bg composite + robust camera func already good. This zero-shot TripoSR with exact per-target pose match is optimal (task-aligned, <1B params, no train). Decision: Update inspect_render.py to test exact correct renderer call (with decoder first), print output dict keys/shapes/types, add conversion to PIL (try common keys, clamp, RGBA), test on sample val obj with rel_az; if succeeds, next port to predict.py + full val test then submit_eval for major LPIPS drop.
